concerns.text: Task T014 mandates fetching stimuli from the 'NAB dataset' (a time-series anomaly detection dataset) to validate visual complexity metrics. The spec requires human-rated background images for meeting contexts. NAB does not contain the required visual stimuli, creating a fatal data source mismatch. The task is not executable as written because the specified source does not contain the required data type.

Task T032 requires downloading meeting clips from 'MeetingBench/meeting-clips-v' on HuggingFace. This dataset ID appears to be hallucinated/non-existent in the provided context. Relying on a non-existent external dataset for the core main study (US-2) blocks all subsequent data collection. The task is not executable because the source URL is invalid.

Task T000 instructs the implementer to 'Update Spec NFR-001' to change the resolution constraint. A task in the implementation phase cannot unilaterally modify the specification document (the source of truth) without a formal change request or a prior approved spec revision. The task is not executable as it violates the spec hierarchy.

Tasks T007-T009 specify 'Unit test for data ingestion... in tests/test_metrics.py' but do not name the specific test function (e.g., 'test_ingest_human_ratings'). This is too coarse for deterministic execution; the atomizer will need to split this. The task should name the exact test function to be created.

Tasks T015-T018 describe tests by description (e.g., 'Unit test for entropy calculation') but do not name the specific test function (e.g., 'test_entropy_calculation'). This is too coarse for deterministic execution. The task should name the exact test function to be created.

Tasks T024-T026 describe tests by description (e.g., 'Unit test for Latin Square counterbalancing logic') but do not name the specific test function. This is too coarse for deterministic execution. The task should name the exact test function to be created.

Tasks T033-T035 describe tests by description (e.g., 'Unit test for Benjamini-Hochberg correction') but do not name the specific test function. This is too coarse for deterministic execution. The task should name the exact test function to be created.

Task T023 implements a fallback to Canny edge detection if YOLO fails. The spec FR-001 strictly defines required metrics as 'image entropy, color variance, and object detection counts'. Canny edge detection produces edge maps, not 'object detection counts'. The task describes an implementation that does not satisfy the spec requirement, making it unexecutable as a valid substitute.
